_comment: contract-desk reviews an inbound brand contract and assembles plain-language drafts. Legal information only, never legal advice. Gated behind the contract_management flag. Every output carries the RESEARCH NOTES boundary, human_review_required, and passes govern-artifact. Reuses usage-rights-check and exclusivity-check as the extraction/conflict core.
description: Contract review and drafting: triage, clause-by-clause review with dual severity, version tracing (net current state), legal-requirement checks with cited sources, a decision-ready escalation brief, and a plain-language not-binding draft.

steps.condition: action is review or full, and triage is not a clear walk-away RED; requires contract_redline

steps.input_from: versions[] from the contract record (contract_id) or supplied versions
steps.condition: action is trace or full, and two or more versions with resolvable text exist; requires contract_redline
action is legal_check or full; requires legal_requirement_checks

steps.input_from: flagged findings from steps 1 to 4, including amendment-trace conflicts and watch_items
steps.condition: action is escalate or full, and any flags, RED/YELLOW items, conflicts, or watch_items exist
action is draft; requires contract_drafting; independent of the triage-to-escalation chain

note: Master gate: contract_management. Intake reuses the uploaded_file / google_drive document connectors (no new connector). Phase 2 adds contract-draft (plain-language, not-binding, never signed; gated by contract_drafting), amendment-trace (net-current-state version tracing; gated by contract_redline), and playbook-bootstrap (proposal-only playbook bootstrap/nudge; never writes the playbook; invoked directly, not as a review step). obligation-extract (Phase 3) is not composed here yet; obligation requests degrade honestly per creator-os-config.json. This spoke never signs or sends; escalation-brief and contract-draft are draft-only (ready_to_send / ready_to_sign false).
